slots.name: weapon
slots.description: Melee or ranged weapon; damage_min/max, speed, weapon_class (1H/2H), damage_type (physical/elemental). Slots in right hand of paperdoll. Layer order z=6 (topmost).
slots.mechanics: Weapon-specific affixes apply (of_dancing, of_tidesong, of_serrated_edge, etc). Rolls from per-item affix_pool. Enchant_chance 5-25% varies by rarity. Speed affects attack rate.

slots.name: armor
slots.description: Body armor (chest piece); base armor + evasion stats. DCSS-faithful slot distinct from helm/boots/gloves. Renders below gloves but above boots in z-order.
slots.mechanics: Generic stat affixes (of_might, of_finesse, of_wisdom). No armor-exclusive affixes. Applies armor value to bot's damage reduction.

slots.name: helm
slots.description: Helmet; armor + evasion focus. Can carry head-slot-exclusive implicit affixes. Topmost visible piece in paperdoll (z=4), sits above gloves and below nothing.
slots.mechanics: Generic stat affixes + helm-exclusive implicits (of_five_heads, of_holy_anchor). Head-slot-only affixes restricted to helm and amulet in applies_to.

slots.name: shield
slots.description: Off-hand defensive item; armor + block chance. Slots in left hand. Layer order z=5 (above armor, below weapon).
slots.mechanics: Smallest generic affix pool (19 affixes). Shield-exclusive mechanic: of_aegis_thorns (generic). Block mechanics drive defensive playstyle.

slots.name: boots
slots.description: Footwear; evasion + movement focus. DCSS-faithful slot. Layer order z=1 (above base, below armor). Can be converted to ring on Octopode/Naga.
slots.mechanics: Generic + evasion-focused affixes. Octopode converts boots→ring (gets +1 ring slot). Spell affixes 30 can apply.

slots.name: gloves
slots.description: Hand armor; dexterity focus, can carry attack-speed affixes. Layer order z=3 (above armor, below helm). DCSS-faithful addition (2026-06-03).
slots.mechanics: Generic stat affixes + glove-exclusive implicits (of_wolf_kinship). Large affix pool (64). Dex scaling + attack-speed bonuses concentrate here.

slots.name: cloak
slots.description: Cloak/cape; worn over armor. DCSS-faithful slot. Layer order z=-1 (BENEATH base sprite, renders as back-layer). DCSS source-accurate rendering (2026-06-05).
slots.mechanics: Generic + cloak-exclusive implicits (of_serpent_growth). Large affix pool (44). Cloaks never render on top of bot despite z-order placement.

slots.name: ring
slots.description: Jewelry slot for stat rings and defensive trinkets. Base slot always present. Species may gain ring2, ring3, ring4 via slot_conversions (Octopode +3 rings via armor/boots/helm conversion, Naga +1 ring via boots conversion). Read by save_state._backfill_equipped_slots as 'ring' (singular).
slots.mechanics: Large affix pool (64). Ring-exclusive mechanic: of_vault_key (archetype, applies only to ring). No visual paperdoll rendering (abstract stat item).

slots.name: amulet
slots.description: Neck slot; stat-focused trinket. Largest per-slot affix pool (83). No visual paperdoll overlay. Read from equipped['amulet'].
slots.mechanics: Largest generic affix pool (83 affixes). Amulet-exclusive implicits (of_phylactery, of_polymorph). Spell affixes can apply to amulets.

slots.name: spell1
slots.description: Autocast spell slot 1; every character spawns with a starter spell here (species-specific). Central to autocast system. Only spell1 guaranteed populated; spell2-5 start empty.
slots.mechanics: Does NOT draw from items.json slot='spell'; instead filled by spell items (234 total) added to inventory/equipped as instances. Managed via doctrine system (BotDoctrine.SPELL_SLOT_IDS). Trigger condition + cooldown per spell defines cast behavior.

slots.name: spell2
slots.description: Autocast spell slot 2; fills from mob drops, chests, shop, boss guarantees. Empty at character creation.
slots.mechanics: Same as spell1: manages autocast spell instances. Doctrine tracks priority/enabled flags per slot.

slots.name: spell3
slots.description: Autocast spell slot 3; fills from loot. Empty at character creation.
slots.mechanics: Same spell mechanics as spell1-2. Doctrine evaluator uses aura_priority array to determine cast order.

slots.name: spell4
slots.description: Autocast spell slot 4; fills from loot. Empty at character creation.
slots.mechanics: Same spell mechanics. All five slots subject to spell_ready (cooldown) predicates in doctrine rules.

slots.name: spell5
slots.description: Autocast spell slot 5 (final); fills from loot. Empty at character creation. Completes the autocast battery.
slots.mechanics: Same spell mechanics. Full affix system applies to spell instances (52 affixes available). Archetype-exclusive flag affixes restrict to specific spell types.

current_slots.notes:
CANONICAL SLOT LIST (save_state._backfill_equipped_slots): weapon, armor, helm, boots, shield, gloves, cloak, ring, amulet, spell1, spell2, spell3, spell4, spell5.

GEAR SLOTS (9): weapon, armor, helm, boots, shield, gloves, cloak, ring, amulet. Items drawn from items.json slot field. Paperdoll renders weapon/armor/helm/shield/boots/gloves/cloak as visual overlays; ring and amulet are abstract (no overlay). Z-order from bottom to top: cloak=-1, boots=1, armor=2, gloves=3, helm=4, shield=5, weapon=6.

SPELL SLOTS (5): spell1-spell5. Do NOT correspond to items.json slot='spell'. Instead, spell instances are items with base_id resolved from items.json (234 spell items total), then instantiated into equipped['spell1..5'] or inventory. Managed by doctrine system (BotDoctrine.SPELL_SLOT_IDS, compile_knobs, cast predicates).

SPECIAL: Octopode/Naga species convert body slots to extra ring slots via slot_conversions: Octopode armor+boots+helm→ring (net +3 rings: ring/ring2/ring3/ring4=4 total), Naga boots→ring (net +1 ring: ring/ring2=2 total). ring_slot_ids() returns full list per species.

AFFIX SYSTEM: Each affix has applies_to array (list of slots where it can roll). 18 affixes apply only to 1 slot (spell archetype-defining flags, ring vault key, glove wolf kinship, etc). Spell affixes=52, amulet=83 (largest), weapon=80, gloves=64, ring=64, helm=59, cloak=44, boots=30, armor=22, shield=19 (smallest).

player_render.render_code:
## EQUIPMENT OVERLAY RENDERING IN BOTTER

### IMPLEMENTATION STATUS: FULLY IMPLEMENTED
Botter renders a complete 7-layer equipment overlay system on the bot sprite in real-time.

---

### RENDERING ARCHITECTURE

**Entry Point: Bot.gd::_refresh_gear_overlays (line 1026-1076)**
- Called from `apply_gear()` (line 565) when equipment changes
- Clears existing overlays, rebuilds fresh sprites each equip swap
- Stores gear sprite references in `_gear_sprites` Dict (slot_id → Sprite2D)

**Core Loop (bot.gd:1038-1062):**
```gdscript
for slot_id in PaperdollRenderer.SLOT_Z.keys():
    var path: String = PaperdollRenderer._resolve_overlay(slot_id, equipped, _items_db_cache)
    if path == "":
        continue
    var sprite := Sprite2D.new()
    sprite.texture = load(path)
    sprite.centered = true
    sprite.texture_filter = CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
    sprite.position = PaperdollRenderer.SLOT_OFFSETS.get(slot_id, Vector2.ZERO)
    sprite.z_index = int(PaperdollRenderer.SLOT_Z[slot_id])
    rig.add_child(sprite)
    _gear_sprites[slot_id] = sprite
```

**Rendering Scope:**
- Overlays are added as children to `rig` (Actor.gd:111), a Node2D that parents the base sprite
- All overlays inherit rig's tweens: lunge animation, death spin, hit squish
- Overlays also inherit rig's facing flip (`rig.scale.x = -1` when moving right)

---

### LAYERING Z-ORDER (PaperdollRenderer.gd:61-72)
```
Layer | Z-Index | Slot        | Description
------|---------|-------------|------------------------
6     | 6       | weapon      | Right hand (top-most, swings)
5     | 5       | shield      | Left hand
4     | 4       | helm        | Head/hat
3     | 3       | gloves      | Hands
2     | 2       | armor       | Body/torso
1     | 1       | boots       | Feet
-1    | -1      | cloak       | Back (behind base z=0)
```

Base sprite (bot.png or species sprite) is at z=0 in rig children order.
Shadow (-1) is added first in Actor._ready (line 119).

---

### SPRITE PATH RESOLUTION (PaperdollRenderer.gd:283-319)

**Flow:**
1. Equipped item instance → `base_id` field (e.g., "iron_sword_5")
2. Look up item definition from items_db by base_id
3. Read item's `overlay` field (explicit) or `tile` field (fallback)
4. Strip extension: "armor_chain.png" → "armor_chain"
5. Construct path: `res://assets/tiles/player/{SLOT_DIR}/{stem}.png`
   - weapon: `res://assets/tiles/player/weapons/iron_sword_5.png`
   - helm: `res://assets/tiles/player/helm/iron_helm.png`
   - armor: `res://assets/tiles/player/body/armor_chain.png`
   - etc.
6. Fallback: If subdir found in stem (e.g., "casters/staff_1"), try basename only

**Coverage by Asset (as of June 2026):**
- weapons/: 131 sprites (all weapon bases)
- body/: 52 sprites (armor)
- helm/: 35 sprites
- shield/: 29 sprites
- boots/: 24 sprites
- gloves/: 11 sprites
- cloak/: 12 sprites

All 7 visible slots have substantial sprite libraries.

---

### POSITIONAL ANCHORING (PaperdollRenderer.gd:49-57)

All overlays render at `Vector2(0, 0)` relative to the rig origin:
```gdscript
const SLOT_OFFSETS := {
    "weapon": Vector2(0, 0),
    "shield": Vector2(0, 0),
    "helm":   Vector2(0, 0),
    "armor":  Vector2(0, 0),
    "boots":  Vector2(0, 0),
    "gloves": Vector2(0, 0),
    "cloak":  Vector2(0, 0),
}
```

**Reason (paperdoll_renderer.gd:28-48):**
DCSS-sourced sprites are pre-aligned by artists on 32×32 canvas:
- Weapon hand grip positioned at the canvas location the player figure's hand occupies
- Helmet centered on player head position
- Boots aligned to player feet
- No per-item offset needed; alignment is baked into the sprite art itself

---

### VISUAL EMBELLISHMENTS

**Rarity Modulation & Glow (bot.gd:1050-1134)**
- `_apply_rarity_decor()`: Modulates sprite color by item rarity (common/rare/legendary)
- Weapon slot only gets glow shader (`item_glow.gdshader`)
- Glow color driven by flavor tag (fire=orange, cold=blue, vampiric=red, etc.)
- Glow pulses via Tween (skipped on web for performance)

**Hand Enchant Ambience (bot.gd:1162-1228)**
- Soft radial glow sprite under weapon hand when equipped item has a flavor tag
- Position: `Vector2(-8.0, 1.0)` relative to rig (DCSS player sprite weapon-hand offset)
- Auto-mirrors on rig flip (left-facing → right-facing movement)
- Pulsing color animation (dim ↔ bright, 1.6s loop)
- Stores in `_hand_enchant_sprite` field; auto-destroyed on re-equip

**Per-Instance Recolor Shader (bot.gd:1179-1192)**
- Items can roll a random hue/sat tint at drop time (stored in `inst.tint` field)
- Applies `item_recolor.gdshader` to overlay sprite (unless glow shader already attached)
- Overlay-specific tint option: `item.default_tint_overlay` overrides `item.default_tint`

---

### WEAPON-SPECIFIC FEATURES

**Stored Reference (bot.gd:398, 1062):**
```gdscript
var weapon_sprite: Sprite2D = null
...
weapon_sprite = _gear_sprites.get("weapon", null)
```

**Swing Animation (bot.gd:1368-1593)**
- 8-directional directional detection: N/S/E/W/NE/NW/SE/SW
- Weapon archetype: polearm (thrust) vs heavy 2H (chop) vs light (slash) vs blade (default sweep)
- Swing tweens rotate, scale, and translate weapon_sprite over 0.3s
- Swing scale multiplier on crits (`_swing_scale()`)
- Example: horizontal slash = rotate ±35° with -110° sweep over 0.08s (line 1466-1483)

**Weapon Trail Emission (bot.gd:1373-1384)**
- On swing, reads weapon's `flavor_tags` from items_db
- Calls `WeaponTrails.emit_burst()` with flavor (fire/cold/vampiric/holy/poison/etc.)
- Spawns particle effect from weapon_sprite position

**Fire Weapon Light (bot.gd:1063-1069)**
```gdscript
if weapon_sprite != null:
    var base_id: String = String(wpn.get("base_id", ""))
    var weapon_light_id: String = String(WEAPON_LIGHTS.get(base_id, ""))
    if weapon_light_id != "":
        LightSpec.attach(weapon_sprite, weapon_light_id, Vector2.ZERO)
```
- Hardcoded light sources for fire-tagged weapons:
  - "firestarter" → "firestarter" light
  - "hellfire" → "hellfire" light
  - "demon_blade" → "demon_blade" light
  - etc. (bot.gd:9-15)
- Light child moves with weapon_sprite during swings

**Crit Flash (bot.gd:1345-1366)**
- White modulate pulse on weapon_sprite when attack crits
- 0.16s tween fade from bright → neutral
- Reuses existing shader/material, only tweens modulate

---

### DATA FLOW: ITEM → SCREEN

1. **Drop / Equip (dungeon.gd)**
   - Item instance created with `base_id`, `enchant`, `tint` fields
   - Instance added to bot.equipped dict under slot key

2. **UI Paperdoll & Live Bot (PaperdollRenderer.build_rig + Bot._refresh_gear_overlays)**
   - Same `_resolve_overlay()` function used by both paths
   - Ensures paperdoll preview matches in-game render

3. **Stat Recompute (StatCalc.compute)**
   - Item affixes folded into bot.damage, bot.armor, bot.crit_chance, etc.
   - Spell augments & color flags (visual_umbral, visual_prismatic, etc.) applied

4. **Combat → Animation**
   - Bot.attempt_attack(): determines crit, updates weapon_sprite modulate
   - Bot.swing_weapon(toward): 8-dir dispatch → swing tween + trail emission
   - Actor.attack_lunge(): lunge tween on entire rig (including weapon)

---

### NOT CURRENTLY RENDERED

**No Overlay Sprites For:**
- ring, amulet (jewelry - no visual assets)
- spell1-spell5 (spells - no equipment visual layer; only HUD bar icons)

**Potential Future Additions:**
- Per-slot offsets (currently all at 0,0) — would allow asymmetric poses
- Shield swing animation (currently static)
- Armor tinting (body recolor based on rarity, rare=gold, etc.)
- Per-frame weapon pivoting during combat (locked at 0° currently)
- Dual-wield visual (currently dual weapons block shield slot)
- Visual effects on worn armor (cracks, glowing runes, etc.)

---

### KEY FILES & LINE REFERENCES

**bot.gd:**
- Line 398: `weapon_sprite` field declaration
- Line 948: `_gear_sprites` field  
- Line 1026-1076: `_refresh_gear_overlays()` — main overlay builder
- Line 1050-1134: `_apply_rarity_decor()` — glow + modulate
- Line 1162-1228: `_apply_hand_enchant_ambience()` — weapon hand glow
- Line 1345-1366: `flash_weapon_crit()` — crit white flash
- Line 1368-1593: `swing_weapon()` + 8-direction swing anims
- Line 9-15: `WEAPON_LIGHTS` dict for fire weapon glow

**paperdoll_renderer.gd:**
- Line 49-57: `SLOT_OFFSETS` (all zeros)
- Line 61-72: `SLOT_Z` layering order
- Line 16-24: `SLOT_DIRS` asset directory map
- Line 80-125: `build_rig()` — builds rig with overlays (used in UI)
- Line 283-319: `_resolve_overlay()` — sprite path resolution
- Line 170-181: `_apply_rarity_modulate()` — color tint by rarity
- Line 182-225: `_apply_rarity_glow()` — weapon glow shader
- Line 238-280: `_apply_hand_enchant()` — weapon hand glow (UI)

**actor.gd:**
- Line 32-37: rig Node2D & sprite Sprite2D setup
- Line 111-113: rig creation & rig.position centering
- Line 185-200: `_set_facing()` — rig.scale.x flip on movement

player_render.notes:
COMPREHENSIVE FINDINGS:

YES, Botter has a fully-implemented multi-layer equipment overlay system.

WHAT'S RENDERED TODAY (7 visible slots):
- weapon (hand-held, swings with combat, emits trails, can glow)
- shield (static at z=5)
- helm (head slot)
- armor (body)
- boots (feet)
- gloves (hands)
- cloak (back layer, z=-1 behind base)

HOW IT WORKS:
1. Item instances stored in Bot.equipped dict (slot_id → instance)
2. Each instance has base_id that maps to items.json definition
3. Item definition includes "tile" field (inventory art) or "overlay" field (explicit)
4. Bot._refresh_gear_overlays() iterates SLOT_Z keys in z-order, resolves sprite path per item
5. Creates fresh Sprite2D children under rig for each equipped slot
6. Applies modulation (rarity color), glow shader (weapons only), hand enchant ambience
7. Weapon sprite specifically stored for swing animation + trail emission

SPRITE PATHS:
- Convention: res://assets/tiles/player/{SLOT_DIR}/{item_stem}.png
- Example: res://assets/tiles/player/weapons/iron_sword.png
- All overlays render at Vector2(0,0) — pre-aligned by DCSS artists on 32×32 canvas
- Fallback: strips subdir if initial lookup fails (e.g., "casters/staff_1" → "staff_1.png")

VISUAL FEATURES:
- Rarity modulation: common/rare/legendary tint on overlay color
- Weapon glow: silhouette-following shader, color by flavor tag, pulsing on non-web
- Hand enchant ambience: soft radial glow under weapon hand, auto-mirrors on facing flip
- Per-instance recolor: hue/sat shader applied to overlay (rolled ~30% at drop time)
- Weapon swing: 8-directional animations with archetype variants (polearm thrust vs heavy chop)
- Crit flash: white modulate pulse on weapon sprite
- Fire weapon light: hardcoded light children on fire-tagged weapons
- Trail emission: flavor-specific particles on swing

ASSET COVERAGE:
All 7 rendered slots have comprehensive sprite libraries (12-131 variants per slot).

NOT RENDERED:
- Rings & amulets (no visual layer, only stat contribution)
- Spells (HUD bar only, no equipment visual)

POTENTIAL ADDITIONS:
- Per-slot offsets (asymmetric poses)
- Shield swing animation
- Armor tinting (currently static)
- Dual-wield visual separation
- On-equipment VFX (cracks, glowing runes, etc.)

atlas_survey.notes: The tile_atlas.json contains 8,477 total tiles with 1,247 tagged as category='item'. Within items, there are 37 named subcategories plus a null category (404 items without explicit subcategory classification). Key findings: (1) Wearable slots are fully represented: amulets, rings, armor (body/chest/torso by weight class), helms/headgear, gloves, boots, shields, cloaks, and special/unique armor like dragon armor and barding for mounts. (2) Wieldable weapons span melee (long/short blades, axes, maces/flails, polearms), ranged (bows, crossbows, slings, blowguns), and specialty weapons (staves, rods, wands). (3) Consumables are well-represented: books, scrolls, potions, and food. (4) Miscellaneous category (75 items) covers keys, lamps, mirrors, bottles, boxes. (5) The dcss directory exists at /Users/dyo/claude/botter/dcss/ with subdirectories: Dungeon Crawl Stone Soup Full and Dungeon Crawl Stone Soup Supplemental. These contain item/, dungeon/, monster/, effect/, player/, gui/, and emissaries/ subdirectories as reference/import sources. (6) Project assets are organized into three main tile subdirectories: artefacts (unique/named items), casters (spell/magical asset graphics), and spells (spell visual effects). No candidate slots are missing from Botter's current wearable inventory—the system comprehensively covers armor, jewelry, and shields."

dcss_conventions.notes: Equipment slots are differentiated between 'real' slots (SLOT_WEAPON through SLOT_GIZMO) that players can directly equip items into, flexible slots (SLOT_WEAPON_OR_OFFHAND, SLOT_HAUNTED_AUX) for special cases, and grouping slots (SLOT_ALL_ARMOUR, SLOT_ALL_JEWELLERY) used for queries. Player overlay rendering is determined by item type: worn equipment (weapons, armour, staves, gizmos) draws on the player sprite; consumables and utilities (potions, scrolls, wands) do not. Jewelry does not render as visual overlays despite being equipped.
